programme.desc: A gang of gold-hunting outlaws, headed by the ruthless Left Eye Watkins (Bobby Brown), takes over the peaceful town of Flatridge, tearing the sleepy outpost to shreds as they search for riches. They murder a can-can girl, who is the sister of Rachel (Monica Calhoun), the onetime leader of a notorious all-girl gang. Rachel then rounds up her four old associates, and they seek revenge for her sister. Using teamwork and a sassy attitude, Rachel's gang goes up against Watkins' tough crew.

programme.title: Edison
programme.desc: Ambitious young reporter Pollack (Justin Timberlake) suspects insidious corruption in his town's elite police unit, known as F.R.A.T., and finds a friendly source in Deed (LL Cool J), a dissatisfied member of the squad. With Deed's information and the backing of his publisher, Ashford (Morgan Freeman), and a crusading lawyer, Wallace (Kevin Spacey), Pollack infiltrates the secretive police cabal. He finds himself in over his head, however, when he discovers the true extent of the criminality.

programme.title: Senior Moment
programme.desc: A retired NASA test pilot learns to navigate love and life again. Starring William Shatner and Jean Smart.

programme.title: Limitless
programme.desc: Facing unemployment and his girlfriend's rejection, writer Eddie Morra is sure that he has no future. That all changes when an old friend gives him a drug that produces enhanced mental acuity. Stoked on the untested chemical, Eddie rises to the top of the financial world and attracts the attention of a tycoon who intends to use him to make a fortune. But terrible side effects and a dwindling supply threaten to collapse Eddie's house of cards.

programme.title: The Code

programme.desc: An officer for a security agency that regulates time travel, must fend for his life against a shady politician

River Phoenix has been living a dangerous lie and Sidney Poitier is the secret agent who forces him to face the hard truth about his parents true identity in this riveting spy thriller.

programme.desc: A telephone company repairman fixes up a system that brings race-results from far away tracks leading to corruption, power and murder.

The sweet-tempered daughter is being courted by several young men who are stunned when her father announces that she cannot marry until her sister does.

programme.desc: A new victim has been chosen to face off against a ravenous shark in a Texas lake.

programme.desc: When her childhood friend Kayla transfers to her college basketball team, Andrea's reunion turns into a nightmare. Kayla, obsessed with being Andrea's one and only best friend, will harm anyone who gets in her way.

programme.desc: A group of virtual hunters unleash a deadly shark in a Texas lake, causing a young couple to fight for their lives during a peaceful fishing trip in a secluded cove.

A mysterious client hires him for a possession case, drawing Dr. Cheon into a series of unexplainable events that challenges his beliefs and revives childhood horrors he's tried to forget.

programme.title: My Mother's Stalker
programme.desc: Old friends gets together for a high school reunion, only for a crime to set off a series of tragic events.

programme.title: Campus Caller
programme.desc: When a university student goes missing, her mother a former cop goes in search for her.

programme.title: Buried Secrets
programme.desc: A cop-turned-writer creates suspicion when she publishes her first novel.

programme.title: The Moon is in Aquarius
programme.desc: On a starry night lit by an Aquarius full moon, Devante and Liz collide at a house party in San Francisco. The spark is instant-and impossible to ignore. Ditching the crowd, they embark on a spontaneous journey through the city's winding streets, iconic views, and hidden gems. With every laugh and shared secret, their bond deepens. But is this love story bound by time-do they only have until dawn?

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Chapman is an ex-marine in Brazil's slums, battling the yakuza outfit who attacked his sister and left her for dead.

programme.title: The Challenger
programme.desc: The Challenger follows the life struggles of a young, Bronx auto mechanic, Jaden Miller. When Jaden and his mother are evicted from their home, he soon finds that boxing may be the key to stop them from living on the streets and the ticket to a better life. With legendary trainer, Duane Taylor, Miller becomes a local hero and captures the attention of a cable network that decides to create a reality show around his goal to fight to the Light-Heavyweight Title.

programme.title: I'm In Love With A Church Girl
programme.desc: A reformed drug trafficker finds unexpected love with a woman guided by strong faith, testing his resolve to leave his criminal past behind.

programme.title: Edison
programme.desc: Ambitious young reporter Pollack (Justin Timberlake) suspects insidious corruption in his town's elite police unit, known as F.R.A.T., and finds a friendly source in Deed (LL Cool J), a dissatisfied member of the squad. With Deed's information and the backing of his publisher, Ashford (Morgan Freeman), and a crusading lawyer, Wallace (Kevin Spacey), Pollack infiltrates the secretive police cabal. He finds himself in over his head, however, when he discovers the true extent of the criminality.

programme.title: No Disrespect
programme.desc: Milk has run the streets efficiently in his father’s absence. After 15 years in prison, his father Bull, has returned to claim what is rightfully his.

programme.title: Platinum
programme.desc: A mysterious man arrives in Houston, and gets the opportunity of a lifetime, when ICE, a pretty hooker with considerable business acumen, gives him the name Platinum and teaches him the pimp game. Platinum quickly becomes the coldest pimp in the city, until his disturbing past catches up to him, and he kills the niece of a very powerful drug dealer, THE WORD. The Word wastes no time aiming his revenge at Platinum, and everyone he knows.

programme.title: Hoodie Girls Gang
programme.desc: Kayla's boyfriend Jamal is jailed and needs $10,000 bail. Desperate, Kayla and her friends plan an armed robbery. However, the heist goes wrong, and the police are on their trail. To make matters worse, Jamal's boss, Sean, demands payment for the drugs Jamal was transporting. Kayla must now free Jamal and escape town before both the police and Sean catch up to her.

programme.title: Untangled Mirror
programme.desc: J. Nicole is a determined mother, entrepreneur, and hairstylist fighting to balance her personal and professional worlds while navigating love, family, and the weight of false perceptions. By day, she runs her salon with pride, presenting herself as confident and successful. But behind closed doors, she faces child custody battles, tense encounters with her ex, and a controlling new partner.

programme.title: Waiting to Process
programme.desc: Four friends—DeShea, Liz, Raven, and Devine—are bound by sisterhood but challenged by the men in their lives. DeShea dates a man with ties to her family, Liz's boyfriend is immature, Raven juggles multiple men, and Devine's husband has a mistress who becomes a deadly threat. As secrets and betrayals test their loyalties, the women must decide if their friendship can survive the chaos.

programme.title: Invasion of the Pod People
programme.desc: When her friends have huge personality shifts, a woman suspects they’ve been replaced by alien invaders.

programme.title: Killers
programme.desc: When a gang buys stolen drugs, they find themselves trapped in a warehouse with vicious assassins.

programme.title: The Young Cannibals
programme.desc: Seven friends accidentally summon a monster when they are tricked into eating human flesh.

programme.title: Super Cyclone
programme.desc: 300 MPH winds threaten America, so a lone meteorologist must battle the elements to stop the threat.

programme.title: The Final Level: Escaping Rancala
programme.desc: An arcade manager has to save her brother after he is transported into a video game.

programme.title: Little Dead Rotting Hood (Edited Version)
programme.desc: For years, the old woman in the woods has secretly kept the wolves of the forest at bay. But when the woman dies, the creatures suddenly attack the residents of a nearby small town. As more and more people turn up dead, the townsfolk discover something far more sinister than wolves lurking in the backwoods, and fight to save their home from evil by waging a bloody battle against the ruthless creatures. But as the wolves begin to overpower the town, only the womans granddaughter may have the key to stopping the bloodbath before its too late.

programme.title: Becoming Evil - Serial Killers
programme.desc: 21st-century America is a technological wonder: cell phones, internet, 24 hour news media, DNA breakthroughs, people on the move, and expanding urban centers. But many of the old problems remain: drug addiction, homelessness, prostitution, poverty, gangs, murder, and serial killing. Starting in the 1990s serial killing in America has transitioned and adapted to the changing world, while at the same time in many ways staying the same. What has changed: There is new forensic technology particularly DNA analysis, better cooperation between law-enforcement jurisdictions, increasing awareness and fascination in the media and on the Internet with the serial killer.Most importantly there are new psychological understandings of the serial killer.

programme.title: The Dark Side Of The Moon
programme.desc: In 2022, a repair crew is sent to fix an orbital weapon but their spaceship malfunctions and ends up heading toward the dark side of the moon. There, in a mysterious, seemingly abandoned space shuttle, a sinister force lies in wait.

programme.title: Cannibal Ferox (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Here it is! The original uncensored, unrated director's cut of one of the most notorious and violent films of all time. Banned in 31 countries, Cannibal Ferox assaults your senses as a group of Americans lost in the jungles of Amazonia experience brutal retribution at the hands of savage cannibals. Shot on location in the savage Amazon wilds of South America, CANNIBAL FEROX is one of the most violent and shocking films ever made. There are at least two dozen scenes of barbaric torture and sadistic cruelty graphically shown. This is the legendary Grindhouse Releasing edition of Umberto Lenzi's infamous classic of graphic horror.

programme.title: Don't Kill It (Edited Version)
programme.desc: An ancient demon terrorizes a tiny Mississippi town.

programme.title: The Slumber Party Massacre (Edited Version)
programme.desc: When Trish (Michele Michaels) decides to invite her high school girls basketball teammates over for a slumber party, she has no idea the night is going to end with an unexpected guest an escaped mental patient and his portable power drill crashing the party in the cult classic, The Slumber Party Massacre.

programme.title: Scare Me
programme.desc: Scare Me

programme.title: Skull: The Mask
programme.desc: An ancient artifact known as the Mask of Anhangá possesses and forces those who wear it to carry out visceral sacrifices in gruesome fashion in this splatter-filled supernatural slasher.

programme.title: Infested
programme.desc: Facing an invasion of venomous spiders, residents of a suburb building will have to find their way out to survive.

programme.title: Skinamarink
programme.desc: Two children wake up in the middle of the night to find their father is missing, and all the windows and doors in their home have vanished.

programme.title: Brooklyn 45
programme.desc: Five military veterans, best friends since childhood, gather together to support their troubled host, and the metaphoric ghosts of their past become all-too-literal.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Culture" - The Clampetts decide to get a taste of culture to fit in the high-class society of Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Needs a Maw" - Mr. Drysdale needs investors for his real estate development project, so he enlists the Widow Fenwick, and aims to cement the deal by hitching her to Jed Clampett, who is looking to find a mother for his daughter, Elly May.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Go Hollywood" - When Jake Clampett tells Jed he's his cousin, he's welcomed into the home and soon has everyone but Jed thinking about and dressing for a role in their next movie.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Are Overdrawn" - The Clampetts receive an overdraft from the bank intended for the deadbeat actor J.D. 'Jake' Clampett, while he gets their statement and starts to spend their money.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Chickadee Returns" - Jethro thinks he's in love with Chickadee Laverne, a stripper he met at the bank. She and the Clampetts have meet and have different ideas about what an "engagement" is, while Miss Hathaway tries to avert this disaster.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro's First Love" - Jethro decides that he wants to go a-courtin', so after Uncle Jed gives him a little education, he finds himself a woman--who happens to be a stripper. As usual, neither she or the Clampetts actually understand what the other is really saying.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampette Look" - Cynthia Fenwick makes her filthy rich mother dress in the "Clampett Look" when they visit the Clampetts, since she thinks Elly is a fashion maven. That only reinforces the Clampett's belief that the Fenwicks are poor.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Starts to School" - Mrs. Drysdale recommends Elly for a high-brow finishing school in hopes that humiliating her will drive the Clampetts away; but instead Miss Hathaway convinces the girls at the school that Elly is a fashion trend-setter. Elly invites the filthy rich Fenwicks over, thinking they're destitute.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Garden" - Grannie has everyone up at the crack of down to plow the front yard for her garden, while Mr. Drysdale, Miss Hathaway, and a stubborn mule try to stop her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Hair-Raising Holiday" - After Granny cuts off the top of Drysdale and Clyburn's (his doctor) hair for some homegrown remedy, Clyburn tries to keep her from ever doctoring again. Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Gets the Misery" - Jed cheers up Granny by pretending to be sick so she can doctor him. Soon Drysdale and his doctor, Clyburn, are subject to her methods as well.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro brings home a friend, Armstrong Dueser McHugh III, who is coddled by a chauffeur who treats him as frail. The Clampetts know better, and show him a good time, friendship and family.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 3 (ME3)-The money bubble has burst, but for seasoned pro Ole Schemion-who has earned over $5 million in Monte Carlo alone-a minimum cash isn't enough. He faces a formidable challenge from French star Gaëlle Baumann and online legend Jussi Nevanlinna at the feature table. With real money now guaranteed, the gloves come off.

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 4 (ME4)-PSPC champion Ramon Colillas surges into the top five as just 40 players remain in the EPT Monte Carlo Main Event. Day 3 intensifies as competitors battle to reach the final two tables. With the finish line in sight, who has what it takes to keep their championship dreams alive?

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 5 (ME5)-The field narrows to just 16 survivors, including EPT Dublin champion Niall Farrell and PSPC winner Ramon Colillas. With over €41,000 already locked up, the stakes couldn't be higher as players fight for their place at the final table of nine. Every hand could be the difference between glory and heartbreak.

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 6 (ME6)-Nine players remain, but only six will advance to the final day with a shot at nearly €1 million and poker immortality. Can PSPC champion Ramon Colillas extend his remarkable run to another major final table, or will his epic journey end just short of the ultimate prize?

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 7 (ME7)-The moment of truth arrives at the EPT Monte Carlo final table. Six players-two businessmen, one qualifier, and three seasoned professionals-wage the ultimate battle for almost €1 million and the coveted EPT trophy. After days of intense competition, only one can claim poker's most prestigious prize.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome Angie Rito and Scott Tacinelli, the chef-owners behind the celebrated New York restaurant Don Angie, for a lively episode dedicated to Italian American comfort food. The conversation begins with admiration for the restaurant’s iconic lasagna before shifting to the main event: a deeply flavorful, “life-changing” meatball recipe inspired by a blend of both chefs’ grandmothers’ traditions. As Angie and Scott walk through their process, they share chef-driven techniques like using a milk-soaked panade for moisture, puréed onions for even flavor distribution, and roasted garlic to avoid harsh raw bites. The hosts jump in with questions and personal anecdotes, sparking a broader discussion about Italian versus Italian American cooking, rule-breaking in the kitchen, and regional food identity—from Cleveland to New York. The group also plays a spirited round of “Discuss,” debating topics like garlic usage, cheese on seafood, and proper spaghetti etiquette, leading to humorous and opinionated exchanges. Along the way, they highlight the philosophy behind Don Angie’s food: bold, unapologetic flavors built on cheese, garlic, and tradition—without strict adherence to old-world rules. As the meatballs finish baking, they’re tossed in a simple, bright tomato sauce and served family-style, earning rave reviews from the hosts for their rich texture and layered flavor. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway of the chefs’ cookbook and a final celebration of Italian American cuisine, emphasizing creativity, collaboration, and the joy of cooking without limits.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon take on the ultimate comfort food by each creating their own version of a grilled cheese sandwich, showcasing three distinct styles that range from classic to elevated. Michael leads with a rich and decadent Croque Madame, complete with a homemade mornay sauce, gruyère, ham, and a perfectly cooked egg on top, sharing tips on building flavor and texture through technique. Carla follows with a Southern-inspired twist, hollowing out a brioche bun to stuff it with pimento cheese and tangy chow chow pickles, emphasizing balance and texture in every bite. Clinton rounds things out with a fan-inspired take on a classic grilled cheese, combining American, cheddar, and gruyère cheeses on rye bread, using mayonnaise for an evenly crisp, golden exterior. Throughout the episode, the trio trades stories, jokes, and cooking insights, debating everything from sandwich ratios to restaurant culture. They later shift into a lively “overrated vs. underrated” game, diving into food trends, brunch culture, and even aging, blending humor with candid reflections. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway and appreciation for sponsors, leaving viewers inspired to experiment with their own grilled cheese creations at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome legendary pitmaster Melissa Cookston for a lively, flavor-packed episode centered around Mexican-inspired smoked chicken wings. The conversation kicks off with playful banter about grilling, live-fire cooking, and Melissa’s impressive barbecue credentials, setting the stage for a masterclass in flavor. Melissa walks the hosts through her approach to wings, emphasizing dry rubs over heavy sauces and showcasing a spice blend inspired by her travels to Mexico, featuring garlic, onion, smoked Hungarian paprika, ancho chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Along the way, the group dives into deeper cooking philosophy—trusting your instincts, cooking with your senses, and understanding that great barbecue is about feel as much as technique. Melissa shares insights on smoking temperatures, wood choices, and why over-smoking can ruin a dish, comparing it to over-salting. The energy stays high as Clinton hilariously spirals into wing-induced chaos, while Carla and Michael debate everything from condiments to air fryers. The episode also highlights Melissa’s new cookbook, Fanning the Flames, described as a love letter to her global culinary travels and the people who inspired her. After cooking, the group digs into the wings, followed by a round of “Chew It or Screw It” and fan mail, blending humor with heartfelt moments. The episode wraps with giveaways and a celebration of community, creativity, and the joy of cooking with fire.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The hosts celebrate their 25th show milestone with guest Chadwick Boyd, who elevates the classic Green Bean Casserole with caramelized onions, fresh beans, and a gruyere drop biscuit topping. While the casserole bakes, the crew debates mashed potato techniques, and Michael shares a hilarious story about how an AI misread his pan wall photo. A fan wins a Caraway pan set.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: A Fan Fridge rescue mission unfolds when a hypothetical Thanksgiving goes wrong. Michael Symon saves the day using Tammy's fridge ingredients to create a savory skillet of chicken thighs, Italian sausage, and sweet peppers. Meanwhile, the hosts share their current TV binges, Michael explains the meanings behind his tattoos, and Clinton sings "Brandy" for a fan. The episode wraps with a giveaway of Carla's measuring bowls.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Guest Danielle Sepsy of The Hungry Gnome joins the kitchen to bake her famous, plush Honey Corn Muffins. As the muffins bake, Clinton and Danielle test Carla and Michael's Long Island accents in a hilarious roleplay game. They also play Chew It or Screw It, share Thanksgiving food disaster stories, and give away a $75 gift card to Danielle's bakery to a fan in Virginia.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Kicking off Thanksgiving week, Clinton Kelly prepares a seasonal Delicata Squash Salad with goat cheese and pepitas. As the crew enjoys the autumn flavors, they discuss Thanksgiving versus Friendsgiving, dive into a comprehensive timeline for holiday prep, and give away a prize to a lucky fan from Oregon.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's Thanksgiving Eve, and Carla Hall takes the stress out of holiday cooking with her fast and flavorful Turkey Scallopini topped with a Cranberry Gastrique. The hosts share their best organizational tips, including Michael's famous oven grid and platter sticky notes. The trio also swaps hilarious stories about cooking under pressure and celebrity encounters.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's the Friday after Thanksgiving, and Chewed Up is all about transforming leftovers into brand-new masterpieces. Carla Hall whips up a decadent Ice Cream Pecan Pie using leftover pie slices, while Michael Symon cooks a savory Stuffing Frittata. The hosts confess their favorite late-night leftover habits, debate mayonnaise brands, and share wild stories about flying with pets.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this Fan Fridge episode, Michael Symon battles a stubborn blender to create a Whipped Cottage Cheese Dip with crispy mortadella chips. The crew embraces the pre-holiday scramble, sharing funny stories about morning commutes and paper cuts.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: During "holiday reset week," Clinton Kelly bakes a festive Stromboli Wreath in a bundt pan. The hosts discuss their vastly different childhoods, play "Holiday Do or Don't," and Michael shares a hilariously horrifying mix-up involving the Hotel Chelsea in New York.

programme.title: Chewed Up

Here the simple and genuine tastes are king.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: A natural haven of tasty delights and local delicacies, the Croix Rousse is Lyon's market gem. From goat farms where the region's most delicious cheeses are contrived to bakeries that produce only organic bread. This episode comes with a warning, be prepared to get very hungry indeed!

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Lying in the culinary heart of Vienna like an island, a little world of treats rises in the midst: the Naschmarkt food market. A throng of buyers and curious onlookers meander through the stalls all consumed by the scent of spices, distracted by the smell of sausage, and stunned by the diversity of turnips, tubers, foliage, and fruit on display. The Naschmarkt truly is Vienna's most popular belly.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Ireland's third-largest city has one of Northern Europe's market gems: The English Market. It supplies high-quality food to affluent English families unlike the Irish market, which no longer exists. The best Irish produce today remains on the island and can be found here.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Perhaps one of the most beautiful and well-organized food market buildings in Europe is the Mercado Central of Valencia. Built on the area where an open-air market stood for centuries, the Valencian style Art Noveau construction is a 'Cathederal of senses'.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The Fish Market or Torget (the square) has a charming location in the heart of the city between the fjords and Bergen's seven mountains. On the harbor, the stalls sell an incredible variety of fresh fish and seafood like urchins, scallops, prawns, and lobsters, but also local farm food.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The market of San Benedetto in Cagliari is the island's belly. It is one of the biggest fresh produce markets in Europe and arguably said to be the biggest fish market in Europe. A paradise for food lovers, where every stall keeper has a story to tell.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Journey to St. John's, Newfoundland, to discover the deep connection locals have with nature's bounty, and meet inspiring individuals who fish, farm, and cook with generations of tradition. Savor unique recipes prepared over crackling fire-pits, including the king of St. John's cuisine: cod.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Join Chef Walter Staib on a journey to Colorado Springs, where we'll explore the history of this scenic landscape, from the indigenous Ute people through the era of homesteaders. At the Historic Rock Ledge Ranch, savor delicious recipes that have withstood the test of time. Don't miss out on this opportunity to experience the flavors and heritage of Colorado Springs!

While attempting to rid the plains of raiders, Sioux Chief Yellow Eagle (Ian MacDonald) and his warriors discover the scene of the massacre. They adopt the sole survivor, a young boy, and rename him Warbonnet. Years later, the adult Warbonnet (Charlton Heston) is in a tough spot when a war erupts between the Sioux and the settlers, prompting the adopted pioneer to question his allegiances.

programme.title: Baby Shark & More Kids Songs - Super Simple Songs
programme.desc: 20 fun Super Simple Songs with Baby Shark, Five Little Ducks, Mr. Golden Sun, Ice Cream Song, and more!

programme.title: Driving In My Car - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Drive a car, ride a bike, row a boat, ride on a train, and fly an airplane with this playful and super fun song for kids; “Driving In My Car”! Driving in my car. Vroom vroom vroom. Driving very fast. Vroom vroom vroom. Driving very slow. Vroom vroom vroom. I like driving. Driving in my car.

programme.title: See You Later Alligator - Super Simple Puppets - Kids Songs - Super Simple
programme.desc: It’s time to go! Here’s a video featuring the Super Simple Puppets for our popular goodbye song, See You Later, Alligator. This is a fun song to sing at the end of a class, party, or play-date!

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Animals
programme.desc: This is all about Animals! Includes children's favorites “Alice the Camel”, “Let’s Go To Zoo” and More!

programme.title: Over The Deep Blue Sea - Puppets - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Yo-ho-ho! Review different types of weather while climbing aboard a pirate ship with the Super Simple Puppets! We’re going this way, that way. Forwards, backwards. Up and down, up and down. Over the deep blue sea.

programme.title: Five Little Chicks - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Five Little Chicks is great for nap time and for practicing counting down from five. Try singing along when it’s time for a nap.

programme.title: Rosey & The Rowdy Rustlers Tour Bus - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Rosey and The Rowdy Rustlers are coming to town to kick off their concert tour! But while driving out on the open road, a giant stray tumbleweed gets in the path of their tour bus, making a mighty mess! Rosey, Roxie, and Rude Jenny will have to take a detour to Carl’s Car Wash! With a quick mess assessment, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Rosey and The Rowdy Rustlers’ vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! The band leaves as happy customers, and just in time for the concert!

programme.title: Who Took The Cookie? (Finny the Shark) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny’s friends are playing a game of Who Took The Cookie From The Cookie Jar. Who do you think took the cookie? The seahorse, the starfish, the octopus, the walrus, the dolphin, or the crab? Let’s play and find out!

programme.title: Moolissa The Underwater Cow - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny’s class finds out they have a new special friend coming for a visit, a cow named Moolisa! Being from land, it can be tricky for Moolisa to do everything that Finny’s friends do in the sea, but Moolisa will try her best to share in all the experiences of life in the ocean.

programme.title: Five Little Monsters Jumping On The Bed - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a silly and slightly spooky version of the classic kids’ rhyme, Five Little Monkeys, with monsters instead of monkeys. It’s a great song for practicing counting down from five, and for teaching little monsters (and children!) not to jump on the bed.

programme.title: Red Light Green Light - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Tobee learned all about traffic safety in school today! He learned how to walk in a crosswalk, to look both ways before crossing a street, and what the colors of a traffic light mean. That reminds Caitie of a song about stopping and going called “Red Light, Green Light.” Together, they sing and dance, stopping and starting again and again. Tobee and Caitie have a blast, and you can join the fun, too! Green light!

programme.title: Twinkle Twinkle Little Star - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Twinkle, twinkle little star. How I wonder what you are?” Lulu the Owl and Juno the Star become fast friends in this Super Simple version of one of the world’s most popular nursery rhymes.

programme.title: Weepy French Onion Soup - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Weepy French Onion Soup. But the four weepy onions they need are VERY tricky to find. Will Humble, Grumble, and Stumble be able to spot all the onions and get back to the kitchen without crying too much? Watch and see!

programme.title: Put On Your Shoes - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a Super Simple Song all about getting dressed! Sing along as we put on our shoes, jacket, hat, and scarf and then head out for a walk with the family.

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Space & Planets
programme.desc: This compilation is about Space & Planets! Includes children's songs "Mr. Golden Sun", “Skidamarink” & "More!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: On a Bermuda Triangle island, Jett and Runi battle a junk monster and restore the beach for Kyle and his dad. Then in Chiang Mai, the team douses flames, reunites baby elephant Paya with her mom, and celebrates by filming their happy day.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A wedding spins into chaos as a giant ring escapes, but Big Wing’s catch saves the day and unveils a joyful bride. Later, Jett and Golden Boy use Cross Combo power to tame a hungry dino plant and rescue Golden Girl and Runi.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett brings a kite to a boy named Akas in the Himalayas, who wants to fly it in a kite festival. Red Pandas take off with the kite and Jett pursues them. When he runs into trouble and gets pinned under a rock, Dizzy shows up with her rescue gear to save the day.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett goes to Venice, Italy with a mask for Luca to wear at his masquerade party. Jett and Luca set out on a gondola to deliver the invitations, but when they run into a waterway traffic jam, Paul must show up to unclog the canal traffic.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Princess Maribelle from England asks for sparkly collars for her puppies to wear at her royal tea party. When the puppies escape and run every which way through the garden, Jett and Jerome join forces to round them up.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Riley from Hollywood is directing his first action film. Jett brings an alien mask and ends up acting as the monster. Riley’s demands get out of hand, so Jerome comes to co-star and help him bring about a peaceful ending.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett takes a pair of running shoes that light up to Lorna in Kenya. She’s going to run in a big race. While training she crosses paths with a lost baby wildebeast. Bello comes to play a game and reunite the baby with its parents.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett has a feathered headdress for Camila from Brazil. She’s dancing the samba in a Carnival parade. Jerome comes to help her with her dance moves and Jett uses his Jett Speed to fix Camila’s float when it suffers a breakdown.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Drew in Canada asks for ice skates so that he can play on an ice hockey team. When he gets to the rink he’s so nervous that he can’t play. Former Mountie Grand Albert gives Drew confidence-boosting tips so he can score the big goal.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Yuri from Russia is part of a family circus. He needs a unicycle for his act on the high wire. The rest of the family is on their way when the circus train breaks down. Jerome performs an amazing act for the crowd while Jett goes to help them.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Karl from Norway asks for a Viking helmet. He goes to Viking Camp where Jett is mistaken for a dragon. The campers chase Jett onto a ship that heads for a waterfall. Mira comes and pushes them away from disaster.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Martine in Paris, France needs a cake-decorating machine. Her father the baker asks her to ice a giant cake for a party at the Eiffel Tower. When the cake is ready the traffic is terrible. Dizzy flies the cake through the sky over the city.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Vietnamese sisters Sen and Kim are fans of pop star Kellie. They ask for wigs like the one their idol wears. When Kellie’s van gets stuck in a rice paddy, Dizzy comes to lead the way to the concert and the sisters are Kellie’s special guests.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett takes brushes to Willem in The Netherlands who is going to paint a mural on a sea wall. When the mural is almost done, a storm approaches. Jett and Donnie borrow the propeller from a windmill to dry the painting before it is ruined.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jerome thinks Jett’s job is easy so he takes his place. He delivers a bathtub to Nayan in Thailand. Boonying the elephant takes a bath but is startled by a spray of water and runs off. Jett arrives to help bring Boonying back.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett carries tires to Alex and his sister Adriana in Monaco. They’ve built a hot dog car for Alex to ride in a soapbox derby. When ducks cause him to run off the course and into town, Paul comes and leads him back with a police escort.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Dive in with your little explorer across the seas to meet amazing animals living in the ocean, coral reef, and polar regions. Overflowing with underwater footage of whales, dolphins, tropical fish, and more, Neptune's Oceans invites you and your baby on an oceanic voyage of discovery!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As each day passes, babies' awareness of the world around them -- and all the wonders it holds -- continues to develop. Wild animals are especially intriguing and World Animals captures the spirtit of little ones' fascination with wild creatures as it introduces them to animals living in the jungle, in the ocean, and on the savannah.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover the exciting world of shapes and colors! Learn about the primary colors red, yellow, and blue with real-life imagery of animals and objects. Explore colors like purple, orange, and green and see artwork by Van Gogh! Discover the circles, squares, and triangles that surround us in everyday life, and introduce baby to the world of visual arts as children and artists create their own work!

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn all about music and rhythm in the world around us! Discover classical music and learn instrument names through narration and repetition. Inspire sweet dreams with gentle melodies and a comforting bedtime story. Learn about classical music from Beethoven, Bach, and Mozart. Sing and dance along to classic rhythmic songs like The Wheels on the Bus and see real-life images of instruments.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Early on in babies' development, they begin to recognize primary colors such as blue, red, and yellow. And all the while, they're also processing an infinite variety of new sights and sounds. Think of the joy you'll experience as you introduce your little one to beautiful music and imagery with Baby Beethoven Symphony of Fun.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Set off on a fun-filled "field trip" with your baby to discover animals found at the park, on the farm, and in your own backyard.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Cal's Sound Yard
programme.desc: Cal's Sound Yard is a new animated series from Baby Einstein that introduces the letters of the alphabet and the sounds they make. Why all the tracing and repetition? And why are the letters out of order? Don't worry! There's a reason for all that; the content is based on "The Einstein Way" - a curiosity-driven learning philosophy, from industry experts, inspired by Montessori and STEAM.

programme.title: Wheels on the Bus Nursery Rhymes For Children - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Wheels on the bus is a nursery rhymes that little babies love! Come join Little Treehouse as they sing, dance in this fun kids educational video with our version of the classic wheels on the bus called the wheels on the bus and many more preschool rhymes for babies like rain rain go away, row row your boat, head shoulder knees and toes and many more

programme.title: Top 10 Nursery Rhymes - Kids TV

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Sonic and Tails befriend a pair of aliens who crash land their spaceship on Mobius. Sonic has to avoid Robotnik's ULTRA-GRAVITIZER (sort of a hedgehog flypaper) to help fix their ship and blast-off back home.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik's momma escapes from the home for mothers of villains and demands her birthday present! When she endangers Sonic and Tails, and they manage to have her taken back to the home, even Robotnik is glad to see her go.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Coconuts is exiled by Robotnik to a remote jungle, where he uses a giant baby ape to destroy villages. But Sonic exposes Coconuts as a robot, and the baby ape's real Dad takes Coconuts and the baby ape away for a spanking.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic is hired into an obvious trap when a frustrated Robotnik decides to appeal to the Hedgehog's ego and announces a "race to determine the fastest on Mobius".

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After Antoine saves Sally's life during a raid, Sonic can't stand his bragging and zips in to see Uncle Chuck, who finds out his "bug" in Robotnik's HQ is malfunctioning.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters, with new addition, Dulcy, develop a De-Roboti-sizer and try it out on Bunnie. It's a success and, against Sally's wishes, Sonic battles Robotnik's newest weapon, a Virtual Reality Shriek-Bot.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After Sonic is almost sucked inside the Void, he finds a huge Ring which Sally believes is an ancient relic but it turns out to be trick of the Sorcerer, Nagus. Sally and Bunnie are drawn inside the Void and when Sonic comes to rescue them.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sonic and The Freedom Fighters take out Robotnik's main computer, they're trapped by Swat-Bots in an underground tunnel, only to be rescued by a handsome teenager named Griff. Griff takes them deep beneath the Big City.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Antoine decides to study martial arts but after he wrecks most of Knothole Sonic sends him off to practice in the woods. //Tails begins his training to become a real Freedom Fighter by learning tracking skills while on a camping trip.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Tails brings a new friend home to Knothole - a baby Terra-Pod who has strayed from the herd that is currently migrating. Sonic, Sally, and some of the other Freedom Fighters return the Baby-T to its mom and the rest of the herd.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sally uncovers Robotnik's plan to seed the cloud with toxic rain, they attempt a daring mission to sabotage the dirigible-like craft being transported by land vehicles. Mission fails when Sally, Bunnie and Antoine are captured.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonia discovers that the watch she received from her friend is actually a monitoring device for the Robotnik Intelligence Agency.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The sinister Robotnik blackmails Cyrus, an old friend of Sonic's, by holding his father hostage.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: On a trip to Port Mobius, Sonic, Manic and Sonia are told that ships are sinking due to the Mobian sea-beast.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Rumor has it that Queen Aleena's journals are buried in the city of Tashistan. Sonic and his siblings search the city for the scrolls.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: In Rainbow Village, people are busy preparing for a dance marathon, a competition in which all of the villagers participate. Ling Ling is very sad that Ellie decided not to participate. Ellie is not a confident dancer so she doesn't want to participate. // Rainbow Ruby's friends tell her that they are now friends with Felicia. They were playing hide and seek but Felicia is such a good hider that she's refused to come out even though she's won the game.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that today is Noodle Festival day in the village but there is a problem. The problem is that the noodle tree, which grows noodles, decided to take a year off and is not growing any noodles this year. // Ling Ling and the friends are worried about the discouraged Gina. Gina just announced that she will stop doing somersaults anymore because she accidentally hurt her friends while practicing.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that Rainbow Village friends were playing around the Rainbow fountain to cool off from the heat but water suddenly stopped coming out of the fountain. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a plumber and starts fixing the fountain. // Kiki's face has turned purple because she ate magical Plumen berries. Kiki accidentally picked magical Plumen berries instead of the delicious rainbow berries she was trying to gather.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ellie's and Jessy's hair are a complete mess after Felicia accidentally causes some Rainbow fruit to fall on them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a hairdresser. // It's winter in Rainbow Village. Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that last night a huge ice mountain appeared so everyone is enjoying an ice sculpture festival. But Jessy seems to have a toothache.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell, Ellie, and Kiki are looking hard for something. It turns out that when Thunderbell was trying on Kiki's crown, he accidentally dropped it and a gem went missing. Kiki sadly says that a princess' crown is worthless without a jewel. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that this year they won't be able to enjoy the traditional rainbow dumplings as the recipe was accidentally torn to pieces. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a chef.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wanted to make a nice garden so she asked the Poppies to do some gardening but they planted the seeds everywhere in the garden, making an absolute mess. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a gardener.// Ling Ling is setting up a 'Thankfulness' tree at the center of the town square. He tells Rainbow Ruby that he is going to hold an event where the village friends can write notes to people that they are thankful to and hang them up on the tree.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are on a train and they are concerned that the train won't stop. The engine is even smoking! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a firefighter. // Paige is extremely upset. It turns out that she's been preparing an awesome fashion show but now it had to be cancelled because Gina accidentally spilled a bunch of fruit onto the stage.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are gathered together at the lake to surf on the Rainbow wave that only comes once a year and they need some help. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a lifeguard and helps her friends safely enjoy their surfing. // Thunderbell is looking extremely worried. It turns out that his pet fish has gone missing. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a detective and finds out Thunderbell's pet fish's whereabouts.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A welcome party for Prince Frederick is taking place in Rainbow Village, but the guest of honour isn't having any fun at all. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a party planner. // The village is a total mess from the paint that spilled from up above. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a clean-up engineer and starts cleaning with her friends.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A Flower Festival is underway in Rainbow Village and Ling Ling has called Rainbow Ruby to help her judge the "best flower" contest. But just as Rainbow Ruby starts looking around at the flowers, Kiki's pet dog Pom Pom starts acting unusual and running around frantically, destroying the festival! // Gina and Jessy are with a huge fish that's injured by the lake. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Marine Biologist. Rainbow Ruby uses her encyclopedia to find out that the fish is actually a dolphin.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The friends are very tired because Mr. Moon hasn't come to sing them their nightly lullaby. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an Astronaut and flies to space to look for Mr. Moon with her friends. // In Rainbow Village, a super frantic Thunderbell is high up in the air, trapped inside a giant bubble. Thunderbell got trapped in a giant bubble in the middle of a bubble bath. In order to find out a way to bring Thunderbell back to earth, Rainbow Ruby turns into a scientist.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina accidentally broke Felicia's house, leaving Felicia homeless. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an architect and builds a house that suits Felicia's demands. // Gina is extremely worried. She needs strawberries for the Rainbow Fruit Ice Cream Festival that's starting soon but the strawberries are all mixed in with other red objects.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell is sad because he's discovered a new fruit and eaten all of it. Now there's none left and he'll never be able to eat that fruit again. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a farmer. // Thunderbell has a terrible tummy ache. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Microbiologist to find out why Thunderbell is sick. Rainbow Ruby uses a microscope to find a bacterium inside the cheesecake Thunderbell had but Felicia accidentally casts a spell that makes the bacterium gigantic.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Cherry Jam is so enjoying staying in Berry Bitty City, that she calls her managers in Big Bitty City and informs them that she is going to stay for a much needed vacation! Strawberry and the girls are thrilled that she will be with them for a little longer, and they do all they can to make their guest comfortable. But Cherry doesn't want to be treated like a guest; she wants to fit in and contribute to the community.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: The Baby Berrykins are enthralled by a fairytale that Blueberry reads to them for Story Time. Charmed by their reaction, Strawberry suggests that the girls do a "berrytale play" for the kids, sort of a "Story Time On Stage." Plum volunteers to direct it. She envisions a small, simple production-until she hears about a gigantic production of "Snowberry and the Seven Berrykins" that's a smash hit in Berry Big City.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry is at Orange's store to mail a birthday card to a friend in Big Bitty City. Just after she drops the card into the mail slot, an ill postal carrier bee sneezes sending envelopes and post cards flying in all directions. The two girls help Jadeybug, a postal clerk, and Postmaster Bee gather up the strewn letters and the ill bee gets sent home from work.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake (Classic)
programme.desc: Strawberry and her friends love to dance - and the Peculiar Purple Pieman of Porcupine Peak is taking advantage of that!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake (Classic)
programme.desc: At the urging of Honey Pie Pony, Strawberry & Friends decide to put on a Cooking Show. Ginger Snap invents a version of TV, which she calls Berryvision, to broadcast the show all over Strawberryland.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake (Classic)
programme.desc: Annie Oatmeal hires both Strawberry Shortcake and Angel Cake to work at her dude ranch. Things get complicated when there's a competition to decide which Ranch Hand runs the Best Bunkhouse.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Raspberry is distraught when she suspects her friends have copied her design on her lantern for the "Glimmerberry Gathering".

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Raspberry is distraught when she suspects her friends have copied her design on her lantern for the "Glimmerberry Gathering".

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Blueberry is disappointed in the girls' lack of enthusiasm for the Patti Persimmon mystery novels, her favorite book series. She finds herself missing the company of her fellow Persimmon fan Huckleberry, and thinks up a great way to get him to visit. She invites Huck to co-write a story with her to submit to an online mystery magazine.. Huck eagerly accepts and comes for a visit.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Berry Bitty City is holding its first city-wide talent show! Hearing that it's the music students of superstar Cherry Jam, a talent scout from Big Bitty City plans to show up. Strawberry and her friends excitedly anticipate her arrival.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Raspberry is discovered by a talent scout who wants her to move to Berry Big City and design a new line of Glitter fashions! She will be provided with her own studio, staff, publicity, commitments from big department stores-everything she needs to be a big fashion designing star! All her friends are thrilled for her. Only Cherry is a bit less enthusiastic about Raspberry's stardom, which Raspberry mistakenly interprets as jealousy.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With the summer almost over, Blueberry overhears Strawberry talking about a wonderful tropical island and believes her friend is going to surprise them all with a much needed vacation! Blueberry makes the other girls promise to keep the secret until Strawberry reveals the big surprise. Of course, even though they've promised to keep the secret, they can't help but try and suss out what the island's like from every little thing Strawberry does.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: It's the last week of summer and Berry Bitty City is hopping with activity as the girls prepare for their tropical island vacation at Berry Breezy Bay Resort. But just as they're about to leave, bad news: the resort is closed for the season due to berry breezy weather. The girls are disappointed, but Orange isn't giving up. She says if they can't go to the tropical island, maybe they can bring the tropical island here!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With only a few days of summer left, the girls are about to enjoy their first vacation in their newly-created, tropical beach resort at Berry Bitty Lake, when who should descend upon them but Mavis Maraschino, the comically over-the-top "Entertainment Tonight"-style show host. Mavis has dumped her focus on fashions in favor of an even glitzier show called Lifestyles of the Berry Big Bitty Stars, and she wants her first segment to be about how pop superstar Cherry Jam spends her vacation.

programme.title: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood
programme.desc: Mister Rogers brings colorful caps, and Mr. McFeely brings a film about how caps are made.

programme.title: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood
programme.desc: Mister Rogers arrives with a small potted oak tree he is looking after for Mr. McFeely.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Sister enters the dog in a pet show. Brother builds a birdhouse instead of tidying.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: The Bear Family find mementos in the attic. The cubs learn their friends are moving away.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: The Bears host a family reunion. Brother and Sister accidentally let cows loose!

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother and Sister adopt a puppy. Brother and Sister damage Ms. Grizzle’s flowerbed.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Papa wants to remove an old tree stump. Then, Brother joins an art class.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother tutors Sister. Papa’s friendship with Farmer Ben is tested.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Papa takes the cubs fishing. Then, Brother gets his own key to the tree house.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother helps with the school talent show. The Bear Family vacations in an old lighthouse.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Mama and Papa let Sister go to a sleep over. Brother complains he has too much homework.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Sister does an assignment on Brother. Sister is jealous of Brother's three-speed racer.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Mama and Papa do a dance demonstration. Brother and Sister love the Space Grizzlies show.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Brother and Sister must clean their room. Then, Papa Bear gets a case of the hiccups!

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Mama takes the cubs outside. Then, Sister gets a haircut.

programme.title: The Berenstain Bears
programme.desc: Mama puts a ban on television for a week. Brother and Sister go trick or treating.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Time for a check-up! Barney helps his friends discover the world of doctors and dentists as they learn about healthy habits like brushing teeth, getting checkups, eating healthy foods and getting exercise.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney has a tee-rific surprise for BJ and Baby Bop. Cousin Riff is moving to town! Riff amazes everyone with his awesome musical abilities. When Barney suggests that they put on a show, Riff becomes shy - he's never performed in a show before. With encouragement from Barney, Riff joins in and does what he knows best - making music!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ brings his brand-new toy plane to the park. Riff borrows the plane while BJ is off playing. Unfortunately, curiosity gets the best of Riff and he ends up taking the plane apart to see how it works. Luckily, BJ isnt mad when he finds out. Instead, he shows Riff how to put the plane back together again. Baby Bop is having a great time playing with her homemade boat. However, what she really wants is to ride in a big boat. Barney takes her to look at boats on the computer while BJ and Riff work on a big surprise for her. When Barney and Baby Bop return to the playground, they discover that BJ and Riff have built a sailing ship on the playset for Baby Bop!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is sad because she lost her pet caterpillar, Monty. Barney and the others try to help her find it. Baby Bop is surprised to discover Monty inside a chrysalis. Everyone is excited to find Monty now a beautiful butterfly! Barney and Baby Bop are thrilled when BJ sets up a bug zoo in the caboose, but Riff isnt so sure he wants to be around bugs. When the park is magically transformed into a amphitheatre with performing insects, Riff discovers that bugs dont bug him after all.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop, BJ and the children prepare for the Shapes Carnival in the park. Riff knows he will do well at the carnival because he is wearing his lucky medal. When he loses the medal, Riff is afraid he wont be able to play the games. With Barneys guidance, Riff has fun playing the games and he realizes he didnt need his lucky medal play and have fun. When it starts to rain at the park, Baby Bop joins Barney and BJ inside the caboose. Baby Bop pokes her head out to see if the rain has stopped and spies a beautiful rainbow in the sky! When the others come to see itits gone. Baby Bop is very disappointed because she liked all the pretty colors, so Barney and the others paint their own life-sized rainbow. When it starts to rain again, the homemade rainbow falls down and gets all wet. Luckily, a real rainbow takes its place!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: An ordinary game of hide and seek takes a not-so-ordinary turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park! Barney and Riff are happy to play along, but BJ thinks that his sister may be seeing things. Fortunately, Barney takes this opportunity to help the young dinos learn about their sense of sight while having tons of fun! BJ and Riff engage in a friendly contest to see which of them can find (or make) the loudest sound in the park. The playful competition continues to escalateand along the way, the sounds of animals, musical instruments, and even fire engines are introduced.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Riff has many special qualities, but sometimes, his shyness makes it hard for him to meet new friends. When Barney introduces Riff to some of his friends in the park, its Baby Bop that ends up feeling less-than-special as the kids seem more impressed with Riff and his special talents. Barney helps Baby Bop realize that theres more to being special than the things you can do. The way you feel and the way you make others feel makes you special, too. Its a special day in the park as Barney and the little dinosaurs enjoy an Art Festival. BJ and Riff share some musical art and Baby Bop shares a painting of a very special friendBarney! As the festival comes to an end, Barney reminds his friends everywhere that no matter what kind of art they make, its always special when it comes from their hearts!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When some of the children in the park invite Barney to join them for a race, BJ and Baby Bop decide hell need their help. They show him several ways to get ready for the big race by warming up and stretching. By the end of the day, everyone finds out that there are many fun ways to exercise. When BJ starts to feel sick after eating too many sweets, he lies down in hopes of feeling better. He has a dream about a snack fantasyland where he eats as many sweets as he wants and ends up with a big stomachache. When he wakes up, hes happy to find out it was only a dream. When he asks for a healthy snack, his friends are happy to oblige.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ and Riff find a pirate map, but in order to find the treasure, they have to be able to count. Since Baby Bop has not learned to count yet, Barney offers to help teach her so she can participate. In the end, Baby Bop learns to count and realizes that feeling good about herself is the greatest treasure in the world! Its time for a music concert in the park. As Riff works on his one-man band, the others make posters for the show. Everyone enjoys Riffs calypso rendition of a favorite song, and they all enjoy helping each other.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Everyone loves Barney! When Ryan tries to be just like Barney, he has a hard time filling Barney's shoes. Ryan soon learns that he is special when he's just being himself! Barney's friends are having a hard time deciding what to do because everyone wants to do the same thing! Barney's Native American tale, The Chase, shows them that it's best to think for themselves and not follow the crowd.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and Riff help Baby Bop learn how to follow the rules and learn that rules make games fun for everyone! Baby Bop is having one of those days! Every time she turns around, someone is telling her no! As her frustration grows, Barney explains that the word no can help you avoid making mistakes or getting hurt.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: There's a fishing contest in the park! When Ryan is tempted not to follow the rules, Barney tells The Emperor's Contest and Ryan discovers that honesty is the best policy. A talented young artist is painting a portrait of Barney, but when she steps away, BJ takes credit for it. When the others beg him to teach them to paint, BJ admits the truth and learns that honesty is the best policy!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: An ordinary game of hide and seek takes a not-so-ordinary turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park! BJ thinks that his sister may be seeing things but Barney takes this opportunity to help the young dinos learn about their sense of sight. When Riff meets a friend who can't hear well, Riff wonders if he would still like music. Barney tells everyone about Beethoven, a famous musician who wrote beautiful music even though he couldn't hear it.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ feels left out when the kids have a Dog Show to show off their pets. BJ doesn't have a dog and wishes he did! A friend shares her dog with BJ so he can join in the fun. Barney sees Riff protecting a nest full of duck eggs. Together, they leave the eggs to look for food for when the ducklings hatch. When they return, Riff is pleasantly surprised to find the eggs have cracked open and the baby ducklings have hatched!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bops blankey seems to be getting smaller; shes convinced its shrinking. But Barney shows her that, actually, she is growing. | Baby Bop isnt satisfied with her skills. She wants to play keyboard like Riff and wants to skate like Barney.

programme.title: Doolittle's Raiders: A Final Toast
programme.desc: The bottle of 1896 Hennessy Cognac was uncorked in front of hundreds of people. The surviving World War II veterans from one of historys greatest military missions were about to raise their silver goblets one last time ending a decades long tradition. It was time for the veterans to hold this final toast. They could wait no longer. Their numbers had dwindled to just a few. Many of the names of the 80 flyers who took part in the April 18, 1942 raid on Japan may not be familiar to most, but collectively they will always be known in history as the Doolittle Tokyo Raiders. Long ago these American flyers, aboard 16 B-25 bombers, had accomplished a daring mission that changed the morale of an entire nation. The aviators, led by famed commander Lt. Col. James Harold Doolittle, had taken the fight directly to the enemy for the first time in the war and delivered retribution for Japans sneak attack on Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941.

programme.title: Eagles of Mercy
programme.desc: EAGLES OF MERCY recounts a seldom-told chapter in the World War II narrative, picking up in the opening moments of D-Day. In the early hours of June 6, 1944, two young American medics with the 101st Airborne Screaming Eagles parachuted into France, and soon found themselves trapped in a 12th-century Norman church in the small village of Angoville-au-Plain. Medics Robert Wright and Kenneth Moore provided first aid to the first casualties of D-Day while a savage battle raged outside between American and German forces. During the documentary, Wright and Moore recount the rigors of basic training, parachuting into Normandy ahead of the amphibious landing on Utah Beach, surviving German anti-aircraft artillery and carrying on their life-saving work even when their unit retreated. EAGLES OF MERCY supplements these compelling stories with newsreel footage, re-enactments and interviews with fellow medics and residents of Angoville.

programme.title: Fannie's Last Supper
programme.desc: Join culinary expert Christopher Kimball and his team of test kitchen sleuths as they attempt to recreate an authentic turn-of-the-century 12-course dinner. For two years, Kimball painstakingly researched and tested recipes from The Boston Cooking School Cook Book, written in 1896 by Fannie Farmer. Tracking down unusual ingredients, discovering forgotten techniques and battling a finicky wood stove are just part of the fun as the pressure builds for the crew to pull off a perfectly-orchestrated meal for their distinguished guests.The project required the team, lead by head chef Erin McMurrin, to constantly regulate the heat on a cast-iron wood stove and work in the Victorian-era kitchen without the benefit of modern appliances. The courses they prepare include: rissoles (filled and fried puff pastry), mock turtle soup with fried brain balls, lobster lAmricaine, roast goose with chestnut stuffing and jus, wood-grilled salmon, roast saddle of venison, Canton punch, three molded Victorian jellies and a spectacular French-inspired Mandarin cake.This special details not only the cooking and serving of the dinner itself, but travels back in time to investigate the life of Fannie Farmer and the world of Victorian cooking, including the foods, the cookware, and the kitchens in an effort to connect the present to the past.

programme.title: We Hold These Truths: The Global Quest for Liberty
programme.desc: This lively documentary explores the promise and global influence of Americas expression of freedom, the Declaration of Independence. Since 1776, more than 100 nations have declared their independence, modeling their declarations on Americas. From Vietnam to Venezuela, from Prague to Hong Kong, millions of lives have been touched by the ideals written in this enduring document. Host Douglas Ginsburg, Federal Judge on the U.S. Court of Appeals, D.C. Circuit, interviews leading experts and ordinary citizens from around the world. In Paris, at the Palace of Versailles, he meets a descendant of the Marquis de Lafayette. In Prague, he tours a cold war bunker and learns that the Czech Republic's journey toward independence mirrors the three great waves of independence that swept the world in the twentieth century. He meets immigrants from Hong Kong, Venezuela, and Vietnam, all seeking the ideals of Life, Liberty and the Pursuit of Happiness. In the United States, Judge Ginsburg travels from Philadelphia to Baltimore to New York and beyond to learn how the words of the Declaration influenced the abolitionist movement, the Women's Suffrage movement, and iconic civil rights figures like Frederick Douglass and Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. WE HOLD THESE TRUTHS is a revealing and fascinating look at this extraordinary and enduring document.

programme.title: Remember Pearl Harbor
programme.desc: The focus of this film is on the military and civilian survivors of Pearl Harbor, all of whom had totally different experiences onDecember 7, 1941.

programme.title: Omaha Beach: Honor and Sacrifice
programme.desc: OMAHA BEACH: HONOR AND SACRIFICE shows the very personal stories of several veterans as they return to Omaha Beach and documents the celebration in Normandy that continues to this day as a result of their acts of courage and determination on June 6, 1944.On D-Day, a roughly 7,000-yard stretch of beach in Normandy, France given the code name Omaha proved to be the Allies' biggest obstacle to the success of Operation Overlord. The assignment to take Omaha Beach, establish a beachhead, and move inland into France was given to two American divisions the already battle-hardened 1st Infantry Division (The Big Red One) and the untested 29th Infantry Division (The Blue and the Gray), who had yet to see any combat in World War II.American Naval Combat Demolition Units also hit the beach that day, writing their own individual stories of horror and heroism. Seven decades after the Boys of Omaha Beach landed, many veterans are returning to this part of the Normandy coast for the last time. Other soldiers who were there on D-Day have also come back for the first time since that historic day, looking for closure as they enter the final years of their lives.

programme.title: War Journal: The Incredible World War II Escape of Major Damon Rocky Gause
programme.desc: The thrilling true story of a nearly impossible escape from prison camp during World War II. In 1942, American airman Rocky Gause escaped a prison camp on the Bataan Peninsula, swam to nearby Corregidor Island, and then sailed with U.S. serviceman William Osborne in a leaky fishing boat 3,200 miles from the Philippines to Australia. Despite facing typhoons, constant threats from Japanese ships, submarines and airplanes, a lack of water and food, and even a visit to the worlds largest leper colony, both men survived to recount their harrowing 52-day ordeal. Filmed in Bataan, Corregidor, and in Manila, the documentary features the journals of Gause and Osborne, archival footage and photos, and interviews with relatives and Army associates. It is narrated by three-time Emmy Award-winning actor Chandler Massey, and actor Kyle Chandler (Argo, Zero Dark Thirty and Carol) provides the voice of Rocky.
